data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on West as Biologics-Driven Double-Digit Growth, Margin Upside and Conservative Outlook Signal Further Upside Potential
data.description:
William Blair analyst Matt Larew has maintained their bullish stance on WST stock, giving a Buy rating on July 15.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Matt Larew has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including West’s ability to deliver another quarter of solid double-digit organic growth, driven largely by strong momentum in its biologics franchise. He highlights that both GLP and non-GLP products are contributing meaningfully, with non-GLP offerings growing at a high-teens rate and GLP-1–related products already representing a notable share of total company revenue.

Larew also points to the company’s top-line results, which exceeded both his and the Street’s expectations, as well as the improved margins and earnings per share performance that underscore operating leverage. In his view, the revised full-year outlook retains an element of conservatism despite a strong first half, leaving room for upside if demand for West’s elastomer and West Vantage solutions continues to track ahead of plan.

According to TipRanks, Larew is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 10.0% and a 53.13% success rate. Larew covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as West Pharmaceutical Services, Option Care Health, and Repligen.

In another report released on July 15, TD Cowen also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $410.00 price target.

data.title: Orr Reiterates Hold on Givaudan as Strong Top-Line Momentum Is Offset by Weaker Cash Generation and Margin Pressure
data.description:
Analyst Ranulf Orr of Citi maintained a Hold rating on Givaudan SA, with a price target of CHF3,150.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ranulf Orr has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Givaudan’s better-than-expected top-line momentum alongside weaker cash generation. The company delivered stronger like-for-like growth, particularly in Fragrance & Beauty and Consumer Products, confirming the resilience of its core franchise, yet this was offset by softer free cash flow and margin pressure despite a modest improvement in gross margin.

At the same time, one-off items such as litigation and project-related adjustments weighed on reported profitability, and higher investment and temporary working-capital needs further dragged on cash conversion. Looking ahead, only mild input-cost inflation, offsetting pricing actions, and the impact of U.S. tariff repayments suggest limited upside to growth forecasts and support Orr’s view that the current valuation already reflects much of the medium-term outlook, justifying a Hold with an expected share price decline of about 6.9%.

In another report released on July 20, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a CHF3,300.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Tesla as Long-Term Autonomy Upside Offsets Near-Term Profitability and Execution Risks
data.description:
Needham analyst Chris Pierce has reiterated their neutral stance on TSLA stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction TSLA bears now have this Tradr ETF

Chris Pierce has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, balancing promising autonomy developments against rising operational concerns. He notes that Tesla’s long-term self-driving narrative gained momentum, as evidenced by stronger customer adoption of FSD and reinforced timelines for initiatives such as Cybercab and Optimus, yet these positives are tempered by nearer-term execution risks.

On the core auto and energy segments, Pierce highlights that demand has recently improved amid higher fuel prices, but profitability still fell short of expectations and competitive threats are mounting, especially as the Model Y meets credible new rivals like RIVN’s R2. He also points to weaker-than-anticipated performance in the Energy business and higher capital spending needs for future products with uncertain returns, leading him to conclude that the current valuation, at roughly 30x his FY30E adjusted EBITDA, reflects a balanced risk/reward profile appropriate for a Hold rating.

In another report released today, Truist Financial also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $370.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: ServiceNow Buy Rating Reiterated on Strong AI Momentum and Resilient Growth; $130 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Bank of America Securities analyst Tal Liani has reiterated their bullish stance on NOW stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Tal Liani has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including robust topline performance and solid AI execution momentum. ServiceNow delivered stronger-than-expected growth in current remaining performance obligations and subscription revenue, signaling resilient enterprise demand and supporting confidence in the durability of its growth outlook.

He also highlights expanding traction in the company’s AI portfolio, with AI-related annual contract value already surpassing $1 billion and progressing ahead of medium-term targets. Liani believes ServiceNow’s unique position as a system of record, combined with its ability to leverage workflow history and contextual data to deliver more efficient and integrated AI solutions than third-party tools, underpins his unchanged $130 price objective and reinforces the Buy recommendation.

Liani covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Cisco Systems, Arista Networks, and ServiceNow. According to TipRanks, Liani has an average return of 9.5% and a 56.20% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Bernstein also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $248.00 price target.
